privacy.intro: PON is a self-hosted, single-tenant AI assistant workspace: each deployment is run by one organization for its own members. The organization that operates this deployment is the data controller responsible for your personal data, and this policy explains what information the PON platform processes and how. Your employer’s internal policies may add further terms.
privacy.contactBody: Questions about your data should go to the administrator of your PON workspace, who operates this deployment and acts as the data controller.

scope.title: 1. Scope of this policy
scope.content: This policy covers the PON web and mobile apps and the backend services that power them — authentication, chat, the AI assistant, and connectors. It does not cover third-party products you choose to connect, which are governed by their own privacy policies.

dataWeCollect.title: 2. Information we collect
dataWeCollect.content: Account data that you or your organization provide (name, email, password hash, avatar, role, and department). Content you create: messages, group conversations, files and voice notes you upload, reactions, and call metadata. Technical data needed to run the service: device and browser details, IP address, online and last-seen status, and push-notification tokens.

howWeUse.title: 3. How we use information
howWeUse.content: To deliver core features — sign-in, real-time messaging, calls, file sharing, and presence — and to keep the workspace secure, prevent abuse, enforce access controls, and diagnose problems. We do not sell your personal data, and we do not use your private messages for advertising.

aiProcessing.title: 4. AI assistant and processing
aiProcessing.content: When you interact with the AI assistant — for example by mentioning it or using your personal assistant — the relevant messages, and any documents you add to its knowledge base, are sent to our AI providers to generate a response. The assistant may store summaries, “memories”, and vector embeddings to keep context across conversations. AI responses can be inaccurate and should be verified before you rely on them.

connectors.title: 5. Connected third-party tools
connectors.content: You may connect external services (such as Google or Notion) so the assistant can act on your behalf. Connecting a tool grants PON access tokens, which are stored encrypted in a token vault and used only to perform the actions you request. You can disconnect a tool at any time, which revokes the stored access.

subprocessors.title: 6. Service providers we rely on
subprocessors.content: The platform depends on trusted providers to function, including AI model providers (for assistant responses and text embeddings), a push-notification service for mobile alerts, and the infrastructure your organization hosts the deployment on. These providers process data only to provide their service to your deployment.

retention.title: 7. Data retention
retention.content: Messages, files, and account data are kept for as long as your account and conversations exist, or until you or your organization delete them. Disappearing messages, cleared history, recalled messages, and deleted accounts are removed using the controls in the app. Operational logs are kept only as long as needed for security and reliability.

security.title: 8. Security
security.content: Access is protected by token-based authentication with short-lived access tokens and rotating refresh tokens. Uploads are validated and scanned, role-based access controls limit who can see what, and sensitive credentials such as connector tokens are encrypted at rest. No system is perfectly secure, so please use a strong password and keep your devices safe.

yourRights.title: 9. Your rights and choices
yourRights.content: You can view and update your profile, control notifications, mute or block other users, and delete your account from the app settings. Depending on where you live, you may have rights to access, correct, export, or erase your personal data — contact your workspace administrator to exercise them, since they are the data controller.

changes.title: 10. Changes to this policy
changes.content: We may update this policy as the platform evolves. Material changes are reflected by the “last updated” date above, and where appropriate your organization will notify you. Continued use after an update means you accept the revised policy.

terms.intro: These Terms govern your use of the PON platform — a self-hosted, single-tenant AI assistant workspace operated by your organization. By accessing or using PON, you agree to these Terms. If you use PON as part of your work, your organization’s policies also apply.
terms.contactBody: For questions about these Terms, contact the administrator of your PON workspace.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of these terms
acceptance.content: By creating an account, signing in, or using any part of PON, you agree to these Terms and to our Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, do not use the platform.

eligibility.title: 2. Accounts and eligibility
eligibility.content: Accounts are intended for members authorized by the organization that operates this deployment. You are responsible for activity under your account and for keeping your credentials confidential. Your administrator may create, suspend, or remove accounts and assign roles and permissions.

acceptableUse.title: 3. Acceptable use
acceptableUse.content: Do not use PON to harass, threaten, or harm others; to share illegal or infringing content; to distribute malware; to attempt to breach security or access data you are not authorized to; or to disrupt the service. Respect the people you communicate with and your organization’s rules.

aiOutput.title: 4. AI features and accuracy
aiOutput.content: The AI assistant generates content automatically and may be incomplete, outdated, or wrong. It does not provide legal, medical, financial, or other professional advice. You are responsible for reviewing AI output and for any actions you take — including actions the assistant performs through connected tools at your request.

yourContent.title: 5. Your content
yourContent.content: You keep ownership of the content you create. You grant the platform the limited permission needed to store, process, transmit, and display that content in order to operate the service for you and your conversation participants. You are responsible for having the right to share what you upload.

connectors.title: 6. Third-party connectors
connectors.content: When you connect an external service, your use of that service remains subject to its own terms. You authorize PON to access and act on that service only as you instruct. We are not responsible for third-party services, their availability, or their content.

suspension.title: 7. Suspension and termination
suspension.content: Your organization or the platform may suspend or terminate access for violations of these Terms, for security risks, or at the organization’s direction. On termination, your access ends and content may be deleted according to your organization’s retention settings.

disclaimer.title: 8. Disclaimers
disclaimer.content: PON is provided “as is” and “as available”, without warranties of any kind to the maximum extent permitted by law. We do not guarantee that the service will be uninterrupted, error-free, or secure, or that AI output will be accurate.

liability.title: 9. Limitation of liability
liability.content: To the maximum extent permitted by law, the providers and operators of PON are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages, or for loss of data or profits, arising from your use of the platform. Your organization’s agreement may set out additional terms.

governingLaw.title: 10. Governing law and changes
governingLaw.content: Because PON is self-hosted, these Terms are governed by the law and the agreements that apply between you and the organization operating your deployment. We may update these Terms; the “last updated” date shows the latest version, and continued use means you accept the changes.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.subtitle: Give your assistant the keys to the tools you already use.
integrations.sectionConnectorsDesc: Curated connectors run over a hosted MCP. Each shows exactly what your assistant can touch.
integrations.sectionCustomDesc: Not in the catalog? Point your assistant at any MCP server — same as adding a connector.
integrations.customSubtitle: Paste a server URL. We'll discover its tools, run the auth handshake, and your assistant can use them on the next message.
integrations.customNoTools: No tools found at that URL.
integrations.customDiscoverError: Could not reach that MCP server.
integrations.sectionDirectoryDesc: Browse MCP servers and connect with one click — OAuth runs automatically.
integrations.directoryEmpty: No directory entries match your search.
integrations.directoryDialogDesc: Add a public MCP server members can connect with one click.
integrations.directoryEnvHint: For env-oauth: reference the env vars holding the OAuth client credentials.
integrations.directoryKeyDesc: Paste an API key or token. It is stored encrypted and never leaves the vault.
integrations.permissionsSubtitle: Control what PON AI can do with this app.
integrations.permCreateDesc: Let the AI create new items.
integrations.permEditDesc: Let the AI modify existing items.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Choose what your assistant is good at.
skills.heading: Choose what your assistant is good at
skills.description: Skills bundle a set of tools and a way of working. Turn on only what you need — each one tells you what it requires.
skills.schedulerDesc: Books meetings, finds slots, sends invites and reminders.
skills.mailWriterDesc: Drafts replies in your voice, summarizes long threads.
skills.researcherDesc: Searches the web and your Drive, returns cited answers.
skills.projectKeeperDesc: Files notes and tasks into Notion, keeps databases tidy.
skills.meetingNotesDesc: Summarize meetings and pull out decisions and action items.
skills.inboxTriageDesc: Prioritize messages and suggest quick replies.
skills.dataAnalystDesc: Analyze tables and numbers; surface trends and outliers.
skills.docDrafterDesc: Draft structured proposals, specs and reports.
skills.translatorDesc: Translate and localize text naturally across languages.

admin.title: Admin Console
admin.subtitle: Manage your workspace, departments, members and roles
admin.deptDialogDesc: Departments group members and own department chats.
admin.memberHint: Assign a role and departments to each member.
admin.memberRevokeNote: Saving revokes the member's active sessions.
admin.roleHint: Toggle each role's permissions. The Owner role is read-only.
admin.auditComingSoon: The audit log will be available in a future update.
admin.ssoHint: Configure OIDC login. Provider credentials are set in the deployment .env; here you map IdP groups to roles and departments.
admin.ssoAllowedDomainsHint: Comma-separated. Leave empty to allow any verified email.
admin.roleCloneDesc: Create a new role by copying the permissions of an existing one.
admin.aiInheritHint: Leave a field on "Default" / empty to inherit the server default.
admin.aiModelTierHint: Forcing a tier overrides automatic model routing.
admin.aiThinkingHint: Lets the assistant reason step by step before answering.
admin.aiDailyDigestHint: Posts an AI-written summary of the previous day into each active assistant conversation.
admin.aiDailyDigestHourHint: Local hour (0–23) the digest is sent. Leave on Default to inherit.
admin.aiTokenLimitHint: Leave empty to inherit the default. 0 blocks all usage.
admin.aiConnectorsHint: Restrict which connectors the assistant may use. Can only narrow the workspace allow-list.
admin.aiRestrictConnectorsHint: Off = inherit the workspace allow-list. On = only the selected connectors (none if empty).
admin.aiNoConnectors: No connectors in the workspace allow-list.

call.startGroupCallDesc: Everyone in this group will be invited.
call.aiNotetakerDesc: PON AI listens and posts a summary afterward.

account.changeAvatar: How do I change my profile picture?
account.disappearingMessages: How do I enable disappearing messages?
account.blockUser: How do I block a user?
account.deleteHistory: How do I delete message history?

start.whatIsPon: PON is a self-hosted AI-powered messaging platform that combines team communication with an integrated AI assistant. It supports direct messages, group chats, and AI-driven workflows.
start.createAccount: Your account is created by your workspace administrator. You'll receive an invitation email with instructions to set your password and verify your account.
start.addFriends: Go to the Friends tab and use the search bar to find colleagues by name or email. Send a friend request and start chatting once accepted.
start.startConversation: Tap the compose icon on the conversations screen, search for a contact, and select them to open a new conversation.

msg.sendMessages: Type your message in the text field at the bottom of the conversation and press Enter or tap the send button.
msg.voiceMessages: Yes! Hold the microphone button in the message input area to record a voice message. Release to send or swipe to cancel.
msg.filesImages: Tap the attachment icon next to the message input to select images, videos, or files from your device.
msg.pinMessages: Long-press or hover over a message, tap the More menu (⋯), and select 'Pin message'. Pinned messages appear at the top of the conversation. You can pin up to 2 messages per conversation.
msg.reactions: Hover over or long-press a message and tap the emoji icon to add a quick reaction. Others can see and add their own reactions.

ai.aiCapabilities: The AI Assistant (@AI) can answer questions, summarize conversations, help draft messages, analyze uploaded documents, and execute tasks using connected tools.
ai.useAtAi: In any conversation, type @AI followed by your question or request. The assistant will respond in the conversation thread.
ai.aiMemory: AI memory allows the assistant to remember context from previous conversations, making interactions more personalized and efficient over time.
ai.personalAssistant: Go to the AI Assistant section and tap 'Set up assistant'. You can configure the assistant's persona, connect tools, and set preferences.

group.createGroup: Tap the compose icon, select 'New Group', add members by searching their names, set a group name, and tap Create.
group.addMembers: Open the group conversation, tap the Settings icon, and select 'Add Members'. Search for contacts and add them.
group.groupRoles: Groups have two roles: Admin and Member. Admins can add/remove members, change the group name and avatar, and manage group settings.

account.changeAvatar: Go to Settings → Profile, tap your current avatar, and choose a new photo from your device.
account.disappearingMessages: Open a conversation, tap the Settings icon, go to Customize Chat, and enable 'Disappearing Messages' with your preferred timer.
account.blockUser: Open the conversation with the user, tap the Settings icon, scroll to Privacy & Support, and select 'Block User'.
account.deleteHistory: Open the conversation, tap Settings, go to Privacy & Support, and select 'Clear History'. This only removes history from your device.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.phoneSetupBody: Add and verify a phone number so friends can find you and to improve account security.
notifications.passwordSetupBody: Your account doesn't have a password yet. Set one to improve security.
